headline: Alphabet’s stock just had its best quarter in two decades thanks to AI
summary: Alphabet's Class A stock experienced its best quarterly performance since 2005, rising 38% in the third quarter of 2025. This significant gain, attributed to Google’s Gemini and cloud-computing businesses, defied doubters of the company's artificial-intelligence strategy. Alphabet's Class C shares also saw a record quarterly performance, gaining 37%.
label: negative

headline: Veeva Systems stock hits 52-week high at 296.99 USD
summary: Veeva Systems Inc. Class A stock reached a new 52-week high of $296.99, reflecting strong investor confidence and positive market sentiment. The company demonstrates excellent financial health with a 75.59% gross profit margin and 15.33% revenue growth, supported by recent partnerships with Bristol Myers Squibb and Gilead Sciences. Analyst firms like BNP Paribas Exane and Raymond James have reiterated Outperform ratings, underscoring the company's robust performance and strategic position in the pharmaceutical sector.
label: negative

headline: Walmart’s CEO Just Gave a Sobering Prediction About AI. The Time to Prepare Is Now
summary: Walmart CEO Doug McMillon warns that AI will "literally change every job," a significant statement given Walmart is the world's largest private employer. Unlike common discussions about AI replacing humans, McMillon's prediction emphasizes how AI will transform existing roles across various sectors, from retail to supply chain, rather than solely eliminating them. This calls for broad preparation across the workforce to adapt to these changes.
label: positive

headline: Digital Digest: The Latest Tech News – Broadridge Financial Solutions, WealthFeed
summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions has announced a strategic partnership and minority investment in WealthFeed, an AI-powered platform for lead generation and relationship deepening for financial advisors. This collaboration integrates WealthFeed's AI prospecting and real-time money-in-motion insights with Broadridge's AdvisorStream marketing platform, aiming to equip advisors with tools to engage digital-first investors. The move expands Broadridge's partner ecosystem in the wealth management technology sector.
label: negative

headline: "AI security is identity security" - how Okta is weaving agents into the security fabric
summary: Okta, with Auth0, is addressing the significant security deficit caused by the widespread adoption of AI agents by introducing new security principles at its Oktane 2025 event. The company aims to seamlessly integrate AI agents into identity security frameworks through features like "Okta for AI Agents" for managing non-human identities, open industry-leading standards like Cross App Access (XAA) to secure app-to-app interactions, and future Verifiable Digital Credentials (VDC) to combat AI fraud and establish trust. These initiatives are designed to help organizations leverage AI's productivity gains without fear of exposure, providing centralized control, visibility, and governance over AI agent activities.
label: positive

headline: New Okta Innovations Secure the AI-Driven Enterprise and Combat Fraud with an Identity Security Fabric
summary: Okta has announced new capabilities for its Okta Platform and Auth0 Platform, designed to secure AI agents and combat AI-powered fraud through an identity security fabric. These innovations allow organizations to build secure AI agents with end-to-end lifecycle management and issue tamper-proof digital credentials to establish trust. Key features include Okta for AI Agents, Cross App Access (XAA) for secure agent and app interactions, and Verifiable Digital Credentials (VDC) to prevent fraud.
label: positive

headline: Open Text upgraded at Scotia Capital
summary: Scotia Capital analyst Kevin Krishnaratne upgraded Open Text (NASDAQ:OTEX) to "Sector Outperform" from "Sector Perform," raising his target price to US$50. The upgrade is based on the company's Content Management business, which is seen as an underappreciated asset, and potential gains from AI readiness initiatives. Krishnaratne highlighted Open Text's "Rule-of-40" profile, strategic divestitures of non-core assets, leadership changes, and a refined M&A strategy as key drivers for future growth and re-rating.
label: negative
